item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really like this. It goes through what should be a perfect, rigorous plan, but it fails, not due to some inexplicable flaw, but worries about that flaw potentially existing.

When you're dealing with the conceptual, ideas itself are relevant actors. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I totally get that now, but I wasn't getting it originally; the ending seemed to come out of left field. Maybe I'm just not getting how significant the flaw in their plan was because of all the esoteric jargon, or how obvious it should have been when the O5s were conceiving their plan.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's meant to be an article about the Foundation being way in over their head. They think they have a grasp on things, they think they can cheat to godhood; they just fail to notice the smallest flaws as they embrace their apparent success.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well that was a wild ride.

Upvote bordering on novote because I feel like the "twist" at the end wasn't properly foreshadowed. Moreover, I'm not really sure why it all failed; the Council was messing with esoteric stuff and seemed to have a handle on things, but they didn't account for subjective reality to screw with it?

It's just a thematic dissonance between "we're so advanced we can measure 4 meters to the quantum level but we’re not advanced enough to predict this massive flaw in our plan".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good concept, middling execution. In particular, I’m not a fan of how much the article relies on 2719 to execute properly, but the detail of giving THRONE-01 third eyes barely compensates for it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…That is an extremely good point lmao
I'll switch that out for something more appropriate

EDIT: switched

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You can't have a "platonically ideal engraving" because the platonic ideal by definition does not have physical existence -__-

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Fascists can't exploit.

Coldposted.

(in the off chance people miss it, the phrases "absolute humanity" and "been secured" contain two crosslinks each)
